swipes.title: Chip War
swipes.description: Chris Miller — Chip War: semiconductors are the new oil, and the world's most critical supply runs through one contested island, Taiwan.

swipes.file: Swipe – Wer bin ich und wenn ja, wie viele – 2026-06-23.html
swipes.title: Wer bin ich – und wenn ja, wie viele?
swipes.description: Richard David Precht — eine philosophische Reise entlang Kants drei Fragen, die große Philosophie mit Hirnforschung verbindet.

swipes.title: A Story-Telling Animal
swipes.description: Philosophize This! on Alasdair MacIntyre — no one is self-made; you can only act once you know what story you're a part of.

swipes.title: Die Thukydides-Falle
swipes.description: Sicherheitshalber Folge 109 — führen Machtverschiebungen wirklich zwangsläufig zum Krieg? Plus eine FCAS-Autopsie.

swipes.title: Sublime Poetry
swipes.description: Raphael: Sublime Poetry at the Met — the first comprehensive US Raphael show, 170+ masterpieces, closing 28 June 2026.

swipes.title: The Coming Wave
swipes.description: Mustafa Suleyman with Michael Bhaskar — The Coming Wave: the DeepMind co-founder argues the AI-and-synthetic-biology wave may be uncontainable, the century's defining dilemma.

swipes.title: Warcraft & Fartcraft
swipes.description: MacroVoices #535 — Michael Every (Rabobank) and Rory Johnston on Iran, inflation, China and the commodity outlook.

swipes.title: A Second Life
swipes.description: Tracey Emin — A Second Life at Tate Modern: a 40-year retrospective, from the bad girl of Britart to one of its great painters of mortality.

swipes.title: Brexit, Ten Years On
swipes.description: Ones and Tooze — a decade after the vote, the economic bill is still arriving, quietly, every year.

swipes.title: The Code of Life
swipes.description: Walter Isaacson — The Code Breaker: a defence system stolen from bacteria became a tool to rewrite our own species.

swipes.title: The Game of Doubt
swipes.description: Freakonomics Radio — an ancient dice game, backgammon, as a school for thinking under uncertainty.

swipes.file: Swipe – The Purpose Put Into the Machine – 2026-06-22.html
swipes.title: The Purpose Put Into the Machine
swipes.description: Brian Christian — The Alignment Problem: how exactly, and what exactly, are we trying to teach machines that learn?

swipes.title: The Wood Wide Web
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Merlin Sheldrake — 'Entangled Life: How Fungi Make Our Worlds, Change Our Minds & Shape Our Futures' (2020). More than 90% of plants depend on fungi they cannot see — and once you take fungi seriously, the line between one individual and another starts to dissolve: 'We are all lichens.'

swipes.title: All Under Heaven
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Sinica — 'To Rule All Under Heaven: Andrew Seth Meyer on the Warring States' (Kaiser Kuo, 21 May 2026). The 260 years between Confucius and the First Emperor wrote the operating system Chinese political life still runs on — and why reaching for Han Feizi to decode Xi is mostly lazy essentialism.

swipes.file: Swipe – I Am Not a Designer – 2026-06-22.html
swipes.title: I Am Not a Designer
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Isamu Noguchi (1904–1988), ahead of the High Museum's design retrospective 'I am not a designer' (Atlanta 2026 → Peabody Essex Museum). Brâncuși's assistant, Martha Graham's set designer, maker of the Akari lamp — a six-decade career that refused every label.

swipes.file: Swipe – Warum es die Welt nicht gibt – 2026-06-22.html
swipes.title: Warum es die Welt nicht gibt
swipes.description: Daily swipe für Simon. Heute: Markus Gabriel — 'Warum es die Welt nicht gibt' (2013). Der Neue Realismus in einem Satz: Es gibt unzählige Sinnfelder, in denen Dinge erscheinen — nur das All-Umfassende, die Welt, gibt es nicht. Aber alles andere schon.

swipes.title: Europäische Katastrophe
swipes.description: Daily swipe für Simon. Heute: Herfried Münkler — 'Der Dreißigjährige Krieg. Europäische Katastrophe, deutsches Trauma 1618–1648'. Ein 975-Seiten-Standardwerk — und Münklers These: kein späterer Konflikt erklärt die Kriege der Gegenwart besser als dieser.

swipes.title: The Survivors
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Charles C. Mann — '1491: New Revelations of the Americas Before Columbus' (Knopf, 2005; National Academies Communication Award 2006). The Americas of 1491 may have held more people than Europe — and what Europeans called wilderness was a depopulated garden.

swipes.title: Great Gun Barrels
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: ChinaTalk — 'Paul Kennedy on Great Powers' (Jordan Schneider, 10 June 2026). The historian of imperial overstretch on Anglo-German antagonism, why Britain made friends with one rising power and enemies with the other, and the uncomfortable echoes for the US–China rivalry of 2026.

swipes.title: Into the Void
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Anish Kapoor at the Hayward Gallery, London (16 June – 18 October 2026). Curated by Ralph Rugoff for his final show as director, the artist's first major UK survey at the Hayward in nearly 30 years — Vantablack voids, mirror sculptures, and a six-metre inflatable that swallows the room.

swipes.title: Power and Progress
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Daron Acemoglu & Simon Johnson — 'Power and Progress: Our Thousand-Year Struggle Over Technology and Prosperity' (Public Affairs, 2023). The book that won the 2024 Nobel team a global hearing: technology has no preordained direction, and shared prosperity is a political choice.

swipes.title: How Progress Ends
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Mark Leonard's World in 30 Minutes — 'How progress ends' with Carl Benedikt Frey (ECFR, June 2026). Computing, the internet, AI — and productivity is still stagnating. The Oxford economist on why stagnation, not innovation, is the historical default.

swipes.title: Carving the Essence
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Constantin Brâncuși at the Neue Nationalgalerie, Berlin (20 March – 9 August 2026, in cooperation with Centre Pompidou). The first comprehensive German Brâncuși show in over 50 years — 150+ works, plus a partial reconstruction of the Paris studio. The man who taught modern sculpture to subtract.

swipes.file: Swipe – The Making of an Icon – 2026-06-21.html
swipes.title: The Making of an Icon
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Frida Kahlo, ahead of Tate Modern's 'Frida: The Making of an Icon' (25 June 2026 – 3 January 2027). How a chronically ill painter from a blue house in Coyoacán became the most reproduced female artist on Earth — and what she lost in the translation.

swipes.title: The Permabear Returns
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Odd Lots — 'Jeremy Grantham on How to Tell If a Bubble Is About to Burst' (Bloomberg, 18 June 2026). The 87-year-old who called 1989 Japan, 2000 dot-com, and 2008 housing says he sees the pattern again — and this time it has a name: AI.

swipes.title: Nature's Last Laugh
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Kyle Harper — 'The Fate of Rome: Climate, Disease, and the End of an Empire' (Princeton, 2017). Rome wasn't brought down by Germans. It was brought down by germs — and by a sky that turned cold.

swipes.title: Waiting for Benedict
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Philosophize This! Ep. 244 — Stephen West on Alasdair MacIntyre's After Virtue (11 Feb 2026). Why modern moral arguments feel unwinnable: emotivism, the failure of the Enlightenment project, and waiting for another St. Benedict.

swipes.title: The Lost Word
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: The Ezra Klein Show — Helena Rosenblatt on 'The Lost History of Liberalism' (5 May 2026). For 2,000 years before the word existed, being 'liberal' meant something very different — and the Cold War quietly stripped it away.

swipes.title: The Dinner-Plate Chip
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Odd Lots with Andrew Feldman — 'Why Cerebras CEO Andrew Feldman Built The World's Largest Computer Chip' (21 May 2026). A dinner-plate-sized chip, a $20B OpenAI contract, and a $56B IPO bet against Nvidia's GPU orthodoxy.

swipes.title: The Lost Virtue
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: The Ezra Klein Show with Helena Rosenblatt — 'The Book That Changed How I Think About Liberalism' (5 May 2026). Liberalism began as a virtue, not a political philosophy. The history was lost. That's why the defence feels so arid.

swipes.title: Born in Bohemia
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Odd Lots — Brendan Greeley on the 500-year history of the dollar. The dollar is older than the United States — and never really belonged to it.
